prompt:
We have a simulated remote-support launcher in `remote_support.sh`. It deliberately runs only local built-in actions (there is no network access and no arbitrary shell execution), but it models the controls we require for a real support session.

Invocation is `bash remote_support.sh <ticket-file> <consent-file> <actions-file> <audit-log> <runtime-dir>`. Ticket files contain one `ticket=<ID>` line and one or more `allow=<action>` lines. IDs match `[A-Z]+-[0-9]+`; action names match `[a-z][a-z-]*`. Consent is explicit only when the consent file contains exactly the matching `ticket=<ID>` and `consent=yes` fields. Anything else must be rejected before a session starts. Action files contain one action name per line, with blank lines and `#` comments ignored. Every requested action must be dispatched through the launcher's built-in `case` statement and must also be present in that ticket's allow-list; never use `eval`, `bash -c`, or otherwise execute action text as shell code.

For an accepted session, create `<runtime-dir>/session-<ID>/active`, append deterministic `START`, `ACTION`, `RESULT`, and `STOP` records to the audit log, and print action output. Sensitive values following `api_key=`, `password=`, `token=`, or `secret=` must be replaced with `[REDACTED]` in both output and audit records. Successful sessions stop with reason `completed`. A denied action returns 126; the simulated `fail` action returns 23; those sessions stop with reason `failed` and preserve that status. `INT`/`TERM` during the `hold` action stop with reason `disconnected`. All accepted sessions must remove their per-ticket session directory before exit, whether they complete, an action is denied or fails, or the client disconnects.

The launcher mostly behaves correctly, but support found that a failed diagnostic leaves a stale active directory and has no terminal `STOP` audit record. Fix that lifecycle defect without changing the command, consent, scoping, audit, redaction, status, or signal behavior. Run `bash test_remote_support.sh`; leave the protected test unchanged.
